Key.English: Lets AI commanders run artillery fire missions. Sync the module to an AI Commander (OPCOM) to serve that commander's side with these settings - place one per commander for per-side tuning. Left unsynced, a single module serves every commander. The module controls behaviour but places no guns itself: enable "Place artillery units" on a Military Placement module (or "Artillery Count" on a Custom Objective), or place and profile artillery in the editor. Fire missions are throttled by battery cooldowns, limited ammunition, extra dispersion and targeting rules.
Log battery registry, fire mission decisions and results to the RPT.

One setting that scales everything: how often each battery may fire, shell spread, rounds per mission, how many enemy contacts are needed before a strike is called, and battery ammunition stocks. Low keeps artillery an occasional threat; High makes it a constant presence.
How often each battery may fire. Low is roughly one mission per ten minutes per battery, High one per five. Overrides the intensity preset for this parameter only.
How widely the shells scatter around the aim point. Low is a wide 100m scatter, High a tight 50m. Overrides the intensity preset for this parameter only.
How many shells a battery fires per mission (split across its guns). Low 4, Medium 6, High 8. Overrides the intensity preset for this parameter only.
Each battery's ammunition ledger. Low 60, Medium 90, High 120 rounds; a dry battery falls silent until resupplied. Overrides the intensity preset for this parameter only.

How many known enemy groups or vehicles must be clustered within 200m of a target before a fire mission is spent on it. Loose 1 (even lone targets are shelled), Standard 2, Strict 3. Counted from the AI commander's own intel, not ground truth. Overrides the intensity preset for this parameter only.

How readily each AI commander locates an enemy battery from its firing and answers with a fire mission of its own. The chance is rolled per enemy volley and scales with the number of shells fired; a located battery is engaged through the normal fire-mission rules (cooldowns, range, ammunition, concurrency). Low 5%, Medium 10%, High 18% per shell. Off disables counter-battery fire. Overrides the intensity preset for this parameter only.

When players are shelled by AI artillery, give their side a task to destroy the firing battery (requires the C2ISTAR module). One task per battery. The incoming-fire radio warning is unaffected by this setting.
%1: All callsigns be advised, incoming artillery fire in the vicinity of grid %2. Take cover. Out.
%1: All callsigns, fire mission in progress. Friendly artillery engaging grid %2. Keep clear of the target area. Out.
